mapping.a_element: conformal transformation of a metric to a Lorentz-Minkowski form
mapping.b_element: Weyl-integrable structure replacing Riemannian geometry to achieve Poincaré invariance

mapping.a_element: conformal factor in the Robertson-Walker metric
mapping.b_element: warp factor or brane thickness profile in the extra dimension

mapping.a_element: maximally-symmetric spacetimes and their conformal forms
mapping.b_element: 4D Poincaré-invariant solution with thick branes in a 5D Weyl-integrable manifold

mapping.a_element: set-theoretical relationships between spacetimes (e.g., Robertson-Walker as a subset of more general spacetimes)
mapping.b_element: hierarchical structure of branes and localization of fields (graviton vs. matter degrees of freedom)

brief_justification: While both papers involve transformations of geometric structures (conformal mappings in A vs. Weyl-integrable geometry in B) and address symmetry or hierarchy problems, their core mechanisms differ fundamentally. Paper A focuses on deriving conformal factors and classifying spacetimes via coordinate transformations, whereas Paper B constructs a non-Riemannian framework to localize fields and resolve mass hierarchy, with no shared dynamical or mathematical machinery beyond generic geometric language.
